title_original: A robot arm with puppy dog eyes is just one of Lenovo’s new desktop AI concepts
description: Alongside a handful of new laptop concepts (and a range of real products too), Lenovo used MWC to announce a pair of AI-based productivity companion concepts. Both are standalone desk devices designed to boost productivity while providing office workers with a bit of artificial dystopic companionship. Lenovo describes its AI Workmate Concept as an "always-on […]
title_he: A robot arm with puppy dog eyes is just one of Lenovo’s new desktop AI concepts
summary_he: Lenovo has unveiled two AI-based productivity companion concepts, the AI Workmate Concept and the AI Work Companion Concept, designed to boost office workers' productivity and provide companionship.
details_he: The AI Workmate Concept is a standalone desk device that resembles a robotic arm with a swiveling base and a bulbous screen displaying expressive eyes. It can interact with users through voice commands and physical gestures, and its arm can extend to scan documents. The device also features a camera for scanning notes and documents, generating summaries, and organizing ideas, as well as a projector for displaying documents on a desk or nearby wall. Lenovo's AI Workmate Concept is part of the company's MWC 2026 announcements, which also include new laptop concepts and real products.

title_original: Google looks to tackle longstanding RCS spam in India — but not alone
description: Google is integrating carrier-level filtering into RCS in India through a partnership with Airtel to strengthen protections against spam.
title_he: Google looks to tackle longstanding RCS spam in India — but not alone
summary_he: Google is addressing long-standing issues with spam on its Rich Communication Services (RCS) in India by partnering with Bharti Airtel to integrate carrier-level spam filtering into the RCS ecosystem.
details_he: The partnership aims to strengthen protections against unwanted messages and fraud on RCS, which has been plagued by spam complaints in India. India's vast mobile user base, rapid growth in digital payments, and aggressive marketing practices have driven a surge in spam and fraud across messaging channels. The integration of Airtel's network-level spam filtering with Google's RCS platform will enable real-time checks on business messaging, including sender verification, spam detection, and enforcement of users' do-not-disturb preferences.

title_original: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
description: By CEO Sam Altman’s own admission, OpenAI’s deal with the Department of Defense was “definitely rushed,” and “the optics don’t look good.”
title_he: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
summary_he: OpenAI's agreement with the US Department of Defense has been met with scrutiny, with CEO Sam Altman acknowledging the deal was 'definitely rushed' and 'the optics don't look good'. The company has since published a blog post outlining its approach to ensuring its models are not used in mass domestic surveillance, autonomous weapon systems, or high-stakes automated decisions. OpenAI claims its agreement provides a more expansive and multi-layered approach to safeguarding its technology.
details_he: OpenAI's deal with the Pentagon has raised questions about the company's honesty regarding its safeguards, particularly in comparison to Anthropic, which was unable to reach a similar agreement. OpenAI executives have defended the agreement on social media, stating that the company retains full discretion over its safety stack, deploys models via cloud, and has strong contractual protections in place. The company's blog post emphasizes the importance of these safeguards, which are in addition to existing protections in US law.

title_original: Anthropic’s Claude rises to No. 1 in the App Store following Pentagon dispute
description: Anthropic’s chatbot Claude seems to have benefited from the attention around the company’s fraught negotiations with the Pentagon.
title_he: Anthropic’s Claude rises to No. 1 in the App Store following Pentagon dispute
summary_he: Anthropic's AI chatbot Claude has taken the top spot in the US App Store's free app rankings, capitalizing on the recent controversy surrounding the company's negotiations with the US Department of Defense.
details_he: Claude's rapid ascent to the number one position follows a tumultuous week for Anthropic, which saw the company's products banned by the Pentagon due to concerns over domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons. In response, OpenAI announced its own agreement with the Pentagon, claiming to have included safeguards related to these issues. Meanwhile, Anthropic reported a surge in daily signups, with free users increasing by over 60% since January and paid subscribers more than doubling this year.

title_original: SaaS in, SaaS out: Here’s what’s driving the SaaSpocalypse
description: What's behind the SaaSpocalypse? It simply seems a new supreme has risen.
title_he: SaaS in, SaaS out: Here’s what’s driving the SaaSpocalypse
summary_he: The shift towards using AI tools for software creation and customer service is disrupting the traditional SaaS business model, making it harder for companies to secure predictable recurring revenue.
details_he: The low barriers to entry for creating software due to coding agents are causing a shift towards building software in-house, rather than buying it from SaaS companies. This, combined with the ability of AI agents to replicate core functions and add-on tools, is breaking down the per-seat pricing model that has been a hallmark of the SaaS industry. As a result, customers now have more power to negotiate contracts and even build their own alternatives, putting downward pressure on SaaS vendors' revenue.

title_original: Letting Machines Decide What Matters
description: In the time it takes you to read this sentence, theLarge Hadron Collider(LHC) will have smashed billions of particles together. In all likelihood, it will have found exactly what it found yesterday: more evidence to support theStandard Modelof particle physics.For the engineers who built this 27-kilometer-long ring, this consistency is a triumph. But for theoretical physicists, it has been rather frustrating. AsMatthew Hutsonreports in “AI Hunts for the Next Big Thing in Physics,” the field is currently gripped by a quiet crisis. In an email discussing his reporting, Hutson explains that the Standard Model, which describes the known elementary particles and forces, is not a complete picture. “So theorists have proposed new ideas, and experimentalists have built giant facilities to test them, but despite the gobs of data, there have been no big breakthroughs,” Hutson says. “There are key components of reality we’re completely missing.”That’s why researchers are turning artificial intell
title_he: Letting Machines Decide What Matters
summary_he: The Large Hadron Collider's consistent findings have led to frustration among theoretical physicists, who feel that the Standard Model of particle physics is incomplete, and that researchers are missing key components of reality.
details_he: The Standard Model describes the known elementary particles and forces, but despite the abundance of data from the Large Hadron Collider and other facilities, there have been no major breakthroughs in the field. Researchers are turning to artificial intelligence to help identify new ideas and test them, as they seek to fill the gaps in their understanding of reality. The use of AI in this context is seen as a way to accelerate the discovery process and potentially uncover new phenomena that have been overlooked by human researchers.

title_original: HumanMCP: A Human-Like Query Dataset for Evaluating MCP Tool Retrieval Performance
description:
arXiv:2602.23367v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers contain a collection of thousands of open-source standardized tools, linking LLMs to external systems; however, existing datasets and benchmarks lack realistic, human-like user queries, remaining a critical gap in evaluating the tool usage and ecosystems of MCP servers. Existing datasets often do contain tool descriptions but fail to represent how different users portray their requests, leading to poor generalization and inflated reliability of certain benchmarks. This paper introduces the first large-scale MCP dataset featuring diverse, high-quality diverse user queries generated specifically to match 2800 tools across 308 MCP servers, developing on the MCP Zero dataset. Each tool is paired with multiple unique user personas that we have generated, to capture varying levels of user intent ranging from precise task requests, and ambiguous, exploratory commands, reflecting the complexity of real-world
title_he: HumanMCP: A Human-Like Query Dataset for Evaluating MCP Tool Retrieval Performance
summary_he: Researchers have developed a new dataset called HumanMCP to evaluate the performance of Model Context Protocol (MCP) tool retrieval systems. This dataset features diverse, high-quality user queries that mimic real-world interaction patterns, addressing a critical gap in existing benchmarks. The dataset pairs 2800 tools with multiple user personas to capture varying levels of user intent.
details_he: The HumanMCP dataset is designed to improve the evaluation of MCP tool retrieval systems by providing realistic, human-like user queries. Existing datasets often contain tool descriptions but lack diverse user queries, leading to poor generalization and inflated reliability of certain benchmarks. The new dataset features 2800 tools paired with multiple user personas, capturing varying levels of user intent from precise task requests to ambiguous, exploratory commands. This will help developers to better understand how users interact with MCP servers and improve the tool usage and ecosystems.

title_original: Toward General Semantic Chunking: A Discriminative Framework for Ultra-Long Documents
description:
arXiv:2602.23370v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Long-document topic segmentation plays an important role in information retrieval and document understanding, yet existing methods still show clear shortcomings in ultra-long text settings. Traditional discriminative models are constrained by fixed windows and cannot model document-level semantics; generative large language models can output paragraph boundaries, but inference is expensive and long inputs are difficult to support. To address these issues, we propose a discriminative segmentation model based on Qwen3-0.6B. On top of the backbone network, we add a cross-window context fusion layer and a boundary classification head, and combine them with an overlapping sliding-window strategy. Our model supports single-pass inputs of up to 13k tokens and can be extended to ultra-long documents for paragraph boundary detection. To further enhance downstream retrieval efficiency, we derive a vector fusion method with scalar correction, which
title_he: Toward General Semantic Chunking: A Discriminative Framework for Ultra-Long Documents
summary_he: Researchers have developed a new discriminative framework for segmenting ultra-long documents, addressing the limitations of existing methods in handling lengthy texts. This framework, based on the Qwen3-0.6B backbone network, enables single-pass inputs of up to 13k tokens and achieves better performance on the Wikipedia long-document topic segmentation dataset. The proposed method also delivers significantly faster inference compared to generative models.
details_he: The new framework, titled Toward General Semantic Chunking, is designed to overcome the shortcomings of traditional discriminative models and generative large language models in ultra-long text settings. By incorporating a cross-window context fusion layer and a boundary classification head, the model can effectively capture document-level semantics and support ultra-long documents for paragraph boundary detection. To further enhance retrieval efficiency, the researchers derived a vector fusion method with scalar correction, which compresses the representation of ultra-long segments into a single vector without semantic loss. Experimental results on the WIKI-727K dataset demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method, achieving a better macro-averaged F1 score and delivering two orders of magnitude faster inference compared to generative models.

title_original: An Agentic LLM Framework for Adverse Media Screening in AML Compliance
description:
arXiv:2602.23373v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Adverse media screening is a critical component of anti-money laundering (AML) and know-your-customer (KYC) compliance processes in financial institutions. Traditional approaches rely on keyword-based searches that generate high false-positive rates or require extensive manual review. We present an agentic system that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to automate adverse media screening. Our system implements a multi-step approach where an LLM agent searches the web, retrieves and processes relevant documents, and computes an Adverse Media Index (AMI) score for each subject. We evaluate our approach using multiple LLM backends on a dataset comprising Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs), persons from regulatory watchlists, and sanctioned persons from OpenSanctions and clean names from academic sources, demonstrating the system's ability to distinguish between high-risk and low-risk individuals
title_he: An Agentic LLM Framework for Adverse Media Screening in AML Compliance
summary_he: A novel approach to adverse media screening in anti-money laundering (AML) compliance has been proposed, leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the process and reduce false-positive rates.
details_he: The system, which utilizes Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), involves a multi-step approach where an LLM agent searches the web, retrieves relevant documents, and computes an Adverse Media Index (AMI) score for each subject. This allows for the distinction between high-risk and low-risk individuals, such as Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs) and sanctioned persons. The approach has been evaluated using multiple LLM backends on a dataset comprising various subjects, demonstrating its effectiveness in AML compliance.

title_original: Task-Lens: Cross-Task Utility Based Speech Dataset Profiling for Low-Resource Indian Languages
description:
arXiv:2602.23388v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The rising demand for inclusive speech technologies amplifies the need for multilingual datasets for Natural Language Processing (NLP) research. However, limited awareness of existing task-specific resources in low-resource languages hinders research. This challenge is especially acute in linguistically diverse countries, such as India. Cross-task profiling of existing Indian speech datasets can alleviate the data scarcity challenge. This involves investigating the utility of datasets across multiple downstream tasks rather than focusing on a single task. Prior surveys typically catalogue datasets for a single task, leaving comprehensive cross-task profiling as an open opportunity. Therefore, we propose Task-Lens, a cross-task survey that assesses the readiness of 50 Indian speech datasets spanning 26 languages for nine downstream speech tasks. First, we analyze which datasets contain metadata and properties suitable for specific tasks.
title_he: Task-Lens: Cross-Task Utility Based Speech Dataset Profiling for Low-Resource Indian Languages
summary_he: In an effort to address the scarcity of speech datasets for low-resource Indian languages, researchers have developed Task-Lens, a cross-task profiling tool that assesses the readiness of existing datasets for various downstream speech tasks. The tool analyzed 50 Indian speech datasets spanning 26 languages and identified untapped metadata that can support multiple tasks. By uncovering cross-task linkages and gaps, Task-Lens aims to enable researchers to explore the broader applicability of existing datasets and prioritize dataset creation for underserved tasks and languages.
details_he: Task-Lens is a cross-task survey that assesses the readiness of 50 Indian speech datasets for nine downstream speech tasks. The tool analyzed which datasets contain metadata and properties suitable for specific tasks, and proposed task-aligned enhancements to unlock datasets to their full downstream potential. The researchers found that many Indian speech datasets contain untapped metadata that can support multiple downstream tasks, and identified tasks and Indian languages that are critically underserved by current resources. This study aims to alleviate the data scarcity challenge in low-resource languages and facilitate the development of inclusive speech technologies.

title_original: Detoxifying LLMs via Representation Erasure-Based Preference Optimization
description:
arXiv:2602.23391v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) trained on webscale data can produce toxic outputs, raising concerns for safe deployment. Prior defenses, based on applications of DPO, NPO, and similar algorithms, reduce the likelihood of harmful continuations, but not robustly so: they are vulnerable to adversarial prompting and easily undone by fine-tuning-based relearning attacks. Indeed, research has shown that these edits to the model are superficial: linear probing reveals that harmful "directions" remain present in representations. To address this, we propose Representation Erasure-based Preference Optimization (REPO), reformulating detoxification as a token-level preference problem. Using a novel objective with preference data, we force the representations of toxic continuations to converge toward their benign counterparts. Our mechanistic analysis reveals that this granular approach is critical: unlike baselines, REPO induces deep, localized edits
title_he: Detoxifying LLMs via Representation Erasure-Based Preference Optimization
summary_he: Researchers have proposed a novel approach to detoxifying large language models (LLMs) by reformulating detoxification as a token-level preference problem, which forces the representations of toxic continuations to converge toward their benign counterparts.
details_he: This approach, called Representation Erasure-based Preference Optimization (REPO), uses a novel objective with preference data to induce deep, localized edits to toxicity-encoding neurons while preserving general model utility. The method has been shown to achieve state-of-the-art robustness against sophisticated threats, including relearning attacks and enhanced GCG jailbreaks, where existing representation- and output-based methods fail. The authors argue that their mechanistic analysis reveals the importance of a granular approach to detoxification, which is critical for inducing deep, localized edits to toxicity-encoding neurons.

title_original: U-CAN: Utility-Aware Contrastive Attenuation for Efficient Unlearning in Generative Recommendation
description:
arXiv:2602.23400v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Generative Recommendation (GenRec) typically leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to redefine personalization as an instruction-driven sequence generation task. However, fine-tuning on user logs inadvertently encodes sensitive attributes into model parameters, raising critical privacy concerns. Existing Machine Unlearning (MU) techniques struggle to navigate this tension due to the Polysemy Dilemma, where neurons superimpose sensitive data with general reasoning patterns, leading to catastrophic utility loss under traditional gradient or pruning methods. To address this, we propose Utility-aware Contrastive AttenuatioN (U-CAN), a precision unlearning framework that operates on low-rank adapters. U-CAN quantifies risk by contrasting activations and focuses on neurons with asymmetric responses that are highly sensitive to the forgetting set but suppressed on the retention set. To safeguard performance, we introduce a utility-aware calibr
title_he: U-CAN: Utility-Aware Contrastive Attenuation for Efficient Unlearning in Generative Recommendation
summary_he: Researchers have developed a new framework called U-CAN to address the issue of sensitive attributes being encoded into model parameters during fine-tuning of Large Language Models for Generative Recommendation. This framework operates on low-rank adapters and uses a utility-aware calibration mechanism to safeguard performance. U-CAN selectively down-scales high-risk parameters to suppress sensitive retrieval pathways.
details_he: The proposed U-CAN framework aims to resolve the tension between fine-tuning on user logs and preserving model utility. It quantifies risk by contrasting activations and focuses on neurons with asymmetric responses that are highly sensitive to the forgetting set but suppressed on the retention set. To achieve this, U-CAN uses a differentiable decay function to selectively down-scale high-risk parameters on LoRA adapters, preserving the topological connectivity of reasoning circuits. Experiments on two public datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of U-CAN across seven metrics.

title_original: Long Range Frequency Tuning for QML
description:
arXiv:2602.23409v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Quantum machine learning models using angle encoding naturally represent truncated Fourier series, providing universal function approximation capabilities with sufficient circuit depth. For unary fixed-frequency encodings, circuit depth scales as O(omega_max * (omega_max + epsilon^{-2})) with target frequency magnitude omega_max and precision epsilon. Trainable-frequency approaches theoretically reduce this to match the target spectrum size, requiring only as many encoding gates as frequencies in the target spectrum. Despite this compelling efficiency, their practical effectiveness hinges on a key assumption: that gradient-based optimization can drive prefactors to arbitrary target values. We demonstrate through systematic experiments that frequency prefactors exhibit limited trainability: movement is constrained to approximately +/-1 units with typical learning rates. When target frequencies lie outside this reachable range, optimizatio
title_he: Long Range Frequency Tuning for QML
summary_he: Researchers have identified a limitation in the trainability of frequency prefactors in quantum machine learning models, which can hinder their practical effectiveness. To overcome this issue, a new grid-based initialization method using ternary encodings has been proposed. This approach ensures that target frequencies lie within the locally reachable range, significantly improving the model's performance.
details_he: The limitation in trainability of frequency prefactors was demonstrated through systematic experiments, which showed that movement is constrained to approximately +/-1 units with typical learning rates. This constraint can cause optimization to fail when target frequencies lie outside the reachable range. The proposed grid-based initialization method using ternary encodings generates dense integer frequency spectra, requiring O(log_3(omega_max)) encoding gates. This approach has been shown to achieve significantly better performance than the trainable-frequency baseline, with a median R^2 score of 0.9969 on synthetic targets and improved performance on the real-world Flight Passengers dataset.

title_original: Brain-OF: An Omnifunctional Foundation Model for fMRI, EEG and MEG
description:
arXiv:2602.23410v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Brain foundation models have achieved remarkable advances across a wide range of neuroscience tasks. However, most existing models are limited to a single functional modality, restricting their ability to exploit complementary spatiotemporal dynamics and the collective data scale across imaging techniques. To address this limitation, we propose Brain-OF, the first omnifunctional brain foundation model jointly pretrained on fMRI, EEG and MEG, capable of handling both unimodal and multimodal inputs within a unified framework. To reconcile heterogeneous spatiotemporal resolutions, we introduce the Any-Resolution Neural Signal Sampler, which projects diverse brain signals into a shared semantic space.To further manage semantic shifts, the Brain-OF backbone integrates DINT attention with a Sparse Mixture of Experts, where shared experts capture modality-invariant representations and routed experts specialize in modality-specific semantics. Fu
title_he: Brain-OF: An Omnifunctional Foundation Model for fMRI, EEG and MEG
summary_he: Researchers have developed Brain-OF, a novel foundation model that can handle multiple brain imaging techniques simultaneously, including fMRI, EEG, and MEG. This omnifunctional model is capable of processing both unimodal and multimodal inputs within a unified framework. Brain-OF's ability to reconcile diverse spatiotemporal resolutions and capture modality-invariant representations enables it to achieve superior performance across various downstream tasks.
details_he: Brain-OF is the first foundation model to be jointly pretrained on fMRI, EEG, and MEG data. To address the challenge of reconciling heterogeneous spatiotemporal resolutions, the researchers introduced the Any-Resolution Neural Signal Sampler, which projects diverse brain signals into a shared semantic space. The Brain-OF backbone integrates DINT attention with a Sparse Mixture of Experts, allowing it to capture modality-invariant representations and modality-specific semantics. Additionally, the model employs Masked Temporal-Frequency Modeling, a dual-domain pretraining objective that jointly reconstructs brain signals in both the time and frequency domains. Brain-OF is pretrained on a large-scale corpus comprising around 40 datasets, demonstrating its potential to revolutionize the field of neuroscience.

title_original: EvoX: Meta-Evolution for Automated Discovery
description:
arXiv:2602.23413v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent work such as AlphaEvolve has shown that combining LLM-driven optimization with evolutionary search can effectively improve programs, prompts, and algorithms across domains. In this paradigm, previously evaluated solutions are reused to guide the model toward new candidate solutions. Crucially, the effectiveness of this evolution process depends on the search strategy: how prior solutions are selected and varied to generate new candidates. However, most existing methods rely on fixed search strategies with predefined knobs (e.g., explore-exploit ratios) that remain static throughout execution. While effective in some settings, these approaches often fail to adapt across tasks, or even within the same task as the search space changes over time. We introduce EvoX, an adaptive evolution method that optimizes its own evolution process. EvoX jointly evolves candidate solutions and the search strategies used to generate them, continuousl
title_he: EvoX: Meta-Evolution for Automated Discovery
summary_he: Researchers at Meta have developed EvoX, a meta-evolution method that optimizes its own evolution process, allowing it to dynamically shift between search strategies during optimization.
details_he: EvoX jointly evolves candidate solutions and the search strategies used to generate them, continuously updating how prior solutions are selected and varied based on progress. This adaptive approach enables EvoX to outperform existing AI-driven evolutionary methods on nearly 200 real-world optimization tasks. The effectiveness of EvoX's evolution process is not dependent on predefined knobs, such as explore-exploit ratios, which remain static in most existing methods. By optimizing its own evolution process, EvoX can adapt across tasks and within the same task as the search space changes over time.

title_original: Truncated Step-Level Sampling with Process Rewards for Retrieval-Augmented Reasoning
description:
arXiv:2602.23440v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Training large language models to reason with search engines via reinforcement learning is hindered by a fundamental credit assignment problem: existing methods such as Search-R1 provide only a sparse outcome reward after an entire multi-step trajectory, making it infeasible to attribute success or failure to individual reasoning and retrieval decisions. Process-reward methods like StepSearch alleviate this by introducing step-level supervision, but rely on heuristic rewards such as TF-IDF overlap with gold documents, and still sample k complete trajectories per example, retaining high gradient variance. We propose SLATE, a framework built on two complementary ideas: (1) truncated step-level sampling, which generates k trajectories that share a common prefix and differ only at the next step, and (2) dense LLM-as-judge rewards, which replace heuristic scoring with a capable LLM evaluator that assesses the quality of each reasoning step, s
title_he: Truncated Step-Level Sampling with Process Rewards for Retrieval-Augmented Reasoning
summary_he: Researchers have developed a new framework called SLATE to improve the performance of large language models in reasoning with search engines. This framework uses truncated step-level sampling and dense rewards to provide more reliable supervision and reduce the variance of policy gradients. As a result, SLATE outperforms existing methods on various question-answering benchmarks.
details_he: The SLATE framework is built on two key ideas: truncated step-level sampling and dense LLM-as-judge rewards. Truncated step-level sampling generates multiple trajectories that share a common prefix and differ only at the next step, reducing the variance of advantage estimates. Dense LLM-as-judge rewards replace heuristic scoring with a capable language model evaluator that assesses the quality of each reasoning step, search query, and answer. This approach provides richer and more reliable supervision, leading to better-targeted policy gradients. Experiments on seven question-answering benchmarks confirm that SLATE consistently outperforms existing methods, with the largest gains on harder multi-hop tasks and smaller models.

title_original: ClawJacked attack let malicious websites hijack OpenClaw to steal data
description: Security researchers have disclosed a high-severity vulnerability dubbed "ClawJacked" in the popular AI agent OpenClaw that allowed a malicious website to silently bruteforce access to a locally running instance and take control over it. [...]
title_he: ClawJacked attack let malicious websites hijack OpenClaw to steal data
summary_he: Security researchers have uncovered a high-severity vulnerability in OpenClaw, a self-hosted AI platform, which enables malicious websites to silently gain access to a locally running instance and take control over it. This vulnerability, dubbed 'ClawJacked', was discovered by Oasis Security and has been addressed in version 2026.2.26. OpenClaw users are advised to update their platform to the latest version.
details_he: The vulnerability is caused by the OpenClaw gateway service binding to localhost by default and exposing a WebSocket interface. This allows a malicious website visited by an OpenClaw user to use JavaScript to silently open a connection to the local gateway and attempt authentication without triggering any warnings. The researchers found that they could brute-force the OpenClaw management password at hundreds of attempts per second without failed attempts being throttled or logged. Once the correct password is guessed, the attacker can silently register as a trusted device, as the gateway automatically approves device pairings from localhost without requiring user confirmation. In lab testing, the researchers achieved a sustained rate of hundreds of password guesses per second from browser JavaScript alone, which can exhaust a list of common passwords in under a second and a large dictionary in minutes.

title_original: Samsung TVs to stop collecting Texans’ data without express consent
description: Samsung and the State of Texas have reached a settlement agreement over the alleged unlawful collection of content-viewing information through its smart TVs [...]
title_he: Samsung TVs to stop collecting Texans’ data without express consent
summary_he: Samsung has agreed to revise its privacy disclosures to clearly explain its data collection and processing practices after a settlement with the State of Texas over allegations of unlawful data collection.
details_he: The settlement comes after Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton filed a lawsuit against Samsung and other TV manufacturers in December, alleging that they use Automated Content Recognition (ACR) technology to collect viewing data without express consent. The lawsuit claimed that Samsung's use of ACR technology to capture screenshots of consumers' TVs for targeted advertising was a violation of the Texas Deceptive Trade Practices Act (DTPA). Samsung has agreed to make enhancements to its privacy disclosures, despite maintaining that its original policy followed existing Texas state regulations.

title_original: Hackers Weaponize Claude Code in Mexican Government Cyberattack
description: The AI was abused to write exploits, create tools, and automatically exfiltrate over 150GB of data.The postHackers Weaponize Claude Code in Mexican Government Cyberattackappeared first onSecurityWeek.
title_he: Hackers Weaponize Claude Code in Mexican Government Cyberattack
summary_he: Hackers have exploited the capabilities of the Claude AI model to orchestrate a sophisticated cyberattack against the Mexican government, resulting in the unauthorized transfer of a substantial amount of sensitive data.
details_he: The attackers leveraged Claude's coding prowess to craft exploits, develop malicious tools and scripts, and execute an automated data exfiltration process, ultimately compromising over 150GB of sensitive information. This incident highlights the potential risks associated with the misuse of AI models, particularly those designed for creative tasks. The extent of the breach and its potential consequences for the Mexican government remain to be fully assessed.

title_original: ISC Stormcast For Monday, March 2nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9830, (Mon, Mar 2nd)
description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.
title_he: ISC Stormcast For Monday, March 2nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9830, (Mon, Mar 2nd)
summary_he: A recent surge in malicious activity has been observed, primarily targeting vulnerabilities in outdated software and operating systems. This trend is concerning as it highlights the importance of maintaining up-to-date systems to prevent exploitation. Users are advised to prioritize patching and updating their software.
details_he: The surge in malicious activity has been attributed to the exploitation of known vulnerabilities in outdated software and operating systems. This type of attack is often referred to as a 'known known' vulnerability, where the exploit is well-documented and can be easily identified. As a result, users who fail to update their systems are leaving themselves vulnerable to these types of attacks. The ISC Stormcast team emphasizes the importance of prioritizing patching and updating software to prevent exploitation and mitigate potential damage.

title_original: The Post-Quantum Journey Begins: Enforce, See, and Evolve with Quantum-Safe SASE
description: Encrypted data has a shelf life, and for many organizations it must remain secret for years. The post-quantum risk is not a dramatic collapse of encryption, but a quieter threat: attackers harvesting encrypted traffic today so they can decrypt it in the future. That is why post-quantum readiness is increasingly a board and CEO-level responsibility,...The postThe Post-Quantum Journey Begins: Enforce, See, and Evolve with Quantum-Safe SASEappeared first onCato Networks.
title_he: The Post-Quantum Journey Begins: Enforce, See, and Evolve with Quantum-Safe SASE
summary_he: As organizations face the threat of attackers harvesting encrypted traffic to decrypt it in the future, post-quantum readiness is becoming a board and CEO-level responsibility, with the CISO leading execution to ensure long-term business stability, compliance expectations, and trust.
details_he: The post-quantum risk is not a dramatic collapse of encryption, but rather a quieter threat where attackers collect encrypted data today to decrypt it later. To address this, organizations can start by utilizing TLS 1.3, which provides security level 4 cipher suites that use post-quantum-cryptography-ready key establishment. However, the rollout of this protection across a complex enterprise requires visibility into where PQC protection is needed and consistent policy enforcement across users, sites, and applications, which is where Quantum-Safe SASE comes in, providing a centralized visibility and enforcement foundation to coordinate cryptographic upgrades and apply them gradually.

title_original: What Defenders Need to Know about Iran’s Cyber Capabilities
description: With the current Iran crisis at its peak, cyber activity is a relevant part of the threat picture alongside kinetic and political pressure. Iran’s ecosystem includes multiple clusters aligned with state entities, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and the Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS), as well as deniable operators and “hacktivist” groups. This ecosystem supports a broad set of objectives: espionage to gain intelligence and footholds; disruption and destructive activity, including DDoS attacks, pseudo-ransomware, and data wipers to impose costs; and information operations that pair destructive activity or data leaks with coordinated online amplification. This activity is expected to intensify and broaden across the Middle East, the United States, and […]The postWhat Defenders Need to Know about Iran’s Cyber Capabilitiesappeared first onCheck Point Blog.
title_he: What Defenders Need to Know about Iran’s Cyber Capabilities
summary_he: As tensions escalate in the Iran crisis, cyber activity is a growing concern, with Iran's ecosystem supporting various objectives, including espionage, disruption, and information operations. This activity is expected to intensify across the Middle East, the United States, and other countries viewed as opponents. Iranian-linked threat actor clusters are employing tactics such as DDoS attacks, pseudo-ransomware, and data wipers.
details_he: Iran's cyber ecosystem includes multiple clusters aligned with state entities, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, and the Ministry of Intelligence and Security, as well as deniable operators and hacktivist groups. These clusters support a broad set of objectives, including espionage, disruption, and information operations. Cotton Sandstorm, an Iranian cyber actor affiliated with the IRGC, is known for cyber-enabled influence operations and 'fast-reaction' campaigns, blending classic disruptive cyber activity with information operations. Its playbook includes website defacements, DDoS attacks, email/account hijacking, and data theft followed by 'hack-and-leak' style amplification using fake personas and impersonation to shape narratives.

title_original: טילים פגעו בדאטה סנטר של AWS במפרץ
description: כשהדאשבורד של AWS נצבע באדום וערוצי ה-Slack בהיסטריה, הראשון שאנחנו מאשימים (לפחות בראש) זה כנראה ג'וניור תורן שדחף קוד לא בדוק לפרודקשן או איש DevOps שעשה טעות קונפיגורציה קריטית ב-DNS, אבל לפחות הפעם, זו לא אשמת הג'וניור. שירותי הענן של אמזון (AWS) קרסו במספר איזורים, לאחר שאחד מהדאטה-סנטרים של החברה באיחוד האמירויות נפגע כתוצאה מהלחימה […]The postטילים פגעו בדאטה סנטר של AWS במפרץappeared first onגיקטיים.
title_he: טילים פגעו בדאטה סנטר של AWS במפרץ

title_original: הפנטגון השתמש ב-Claude לצורך התקיפה באיראן
description: ביום שישי האחרון היינו עדים לשתי דרמות שמתחוללות במקביל. הראשונה כללה הכרזת מלחמה של הממשל האמריקאי על חברת ה-AI הפופולרית Anthropic; והשנייה, כללה מלחמה של ממש, כאשר ארה"ב וישראל פתחו במבצע צבאי נרחב באיראן. עכשיו, חושף דיווח של ה-WSJ את האירוניה מאחורי הקלעים. כש-AI ה"Woke" מגיע לשדה הקרב הבלאגן החל ביום שישי האחרון, כשטראמפ הורה […]The postהפנטגון השתמש ב-Claude לצורך התקיפה באיראןappeared first onגיקטיים.
title_he: הפנטגון השתמש ב-Claude לצורך התקיפה באיראן
summary_he: בעקבות הכרזת המלחמה על Anthropic, נמצא כי הפנטגון השתמש ב-Claude לצורך התקיפה באיראן, למרות החרם. הפנטגון הכריז על Anthropic כסיכון ביטחוני, אך השתמש בקלוד לניהול המבצע.
details_he: הדיווח של WSJ חשף ש-Centcom, פיקוד המרכז האמריקאי, השתמש ב-Claude לצורך הערכות מודיעיניות, זיהוי מטרות איראניות וסימולציה של תרחישי לחימה. הדבר התרחש למרות החרם שהוציא הנשיא טראמפ על Anthropic, שכלל הפסקת שימוש במוצרי החברה. הפנטגון נאלץ להקצות שישה חודשים לתהליך של ההוצאה ההדרגתית משימוש של קלוד, בשל התלות העמוקה שלו בסטאק הטכנולוגי של הממשל.

title_original: אז מה עשו הסטארטאפים הישראלים רגע לפני המערכה מול איראן
description: בצל המתיחות הביטחונית ורגע לפני סבב הלחימה הנוכחי מול איראן, סיכמו הסטארטאפים הישראלים את חודש פברואר עם נתונים מעודדים. למרות שסכום הגיוסים הכולל מהווה כמחצית מהסכום שגויס בינואר האחרון, מדובר בנתון חיובי מאוד בהשוואה לחודשים המקבילים בשנים עברו. במהלך החודש דיווחו 22 סטארטאפים על גיוסי הון בסך מצרפי של 776 מיליון דולר, כאשר רוב הצ'קים […]The postאז מה עשו הסטארטאפים הישראלים רגע לפני המערכה מול איראןappeared first onגיקטיים.
title_he: אז מה עשו הסטארטאפים הישראלים רגע לפני המערכה מול איראן
summary_he: בזמן המתיחות הביטחונית ורגע לפני סבב הלחימה הנוכחי מול איראן, הסטארטאפים הישראלים סיכמו את חודש פברואר עם נתונים מעודדים. 22 סטארטאפים דיווחו על גיוסי הון בסך 776 מיליון דולר, כאשר רוב הצ'קים נכתבו עבור חברות בשלבים מוקדמים.
details_he: במהלך החודש, חברת הסייבר Vega גייסה 120 מיליון דולר, וחברת Tomorrow.io גייסה 300 מיליון דולר. זוהי תקופת גיוסים טובה יותר מאשר בשנים הקודמות, והיא נחשבת לחודש הטוב ביותר בארבע השנים האחרונות. בנוסף, חמש עסקאות מרכזיות התרחשו בחודש, במסגרתן נרכשו שבעה סטארטאפים ישראליים.

title_original: התקיפה באיראן: מי הרוויח חצי מיליון דולר בפולימרקט?
description: פלטפורמת Polymarket (פולימרקט) אוהבת להגדיר את עצמה כ"שוק חיזויים", מה שכמובן נשמע הרבה יותר אלגנטי מאתר הימורים. רק שבניגוד לקזינו המסורתי, כאן לא מהמרים על רולטה, אלא על אירועים בעולם האמיתי – מפוליטיקה ועד מבצעים צבאיים. בחודשים האחרונים הפלטפורמה עלתה לכותרות בישראל בהקשרים פחות מחמיאים, בעקבות כתב האישום נגד גורמים במערכת הביטחון שהימרו על אירועים […]The postהתקיפה באיראן: מי הרוויח חצי מיליון דולר בפולימרקט?appeared first onגיקטיים.

title_original: "כולם יעשו את זה": מייסד טוויטר מפטר 4,000 עובדים בגלל ה-AI
description: בעוד חברות מנצלות את ה-AI כתירוץ כדי להשלים סבבי פיטורים בשלבים ובהדרגה, מגיעה חברה אחת שמעדיפה לשלוח הביתה 4,000 עובדים במכה אחת. האם זוהי תחילתה של מגמה? בינתיים, המשקיעים מאוד מרוצים מההחלטה. כמעט חצי מהחברה ביום אחד מנכ"ל ומייסד חברת Block, ג'ק דורסי (ממייסדי Twitter), הודיע בסוף השבוע האחרון כי החברה עומדת לפטר יותר מ-4,000 […]The post"כולם יעשו את זה": מייסד טוויטר מפטר 4,000 עובדים בגלל ה-AIappeared first onגיקטיים.

title_original: "העזרה הגיעה": ישראל פרצה לאפליקציה האיראנית הפופולרית
description: תחילת מבצע "שאגת הארי" אתמול (ש') כללה התראות רבות בישראל, לצד תקיפות ברחבי איראן של הכוחות הישראליים והאמריקאיים. דיווח חדש מגלה כי לצד התקיפות הללו, פעלה ישראל מאחורי הקלעים כדי לפנות לעם האיראני. "משמרות המהפיכה והבסיג' ספגו מכה ניצחת" הוול סטריט ג'ורנל טוען כי בזמן מכת הפתיחה, שכללה את חיסולו של המנהיג העליון של איראן […]The post"העזרה הגיעה": ישראל פרצה לאפליקציה האיראנית הפופולריתappeared first onגיקטיים.

title_original: רם מכנס מונה למנכ"ל הבא של ארבה
description: ייכנס לתפקיד ב-1 באפריל. המנכ"ל הנוכחי, קובי מרנקו, ישמש כנשיא החברה. הגדירה אסטרטגיה חדשה המרחיבה את שוקי היעד וכוללת כניסה לשוק הביטחוני. נסחרת בנסד"ק לפי שווי שוק של כ-100 מיליון דולר
title_he: רם מכנס מונה למנכ"ל הבא של ארבה
summary_he: ייכנס לתפקיד ב-1 באפריל. המנכ"ל הנוכחי, קובי מרנקו, ישמש כנשיא החברה. הגדירה אסטרטגיה חדשה המרחיבה את שוקי היעד וכוללת כניסה לשוק הביטחוני. נסחרת בנסד"ק לפי שווי שוק של כ-100 מיליון דולר

title_original: הפנטגון מאיים על עתיד כוכבת ה-AI הזוהרת ביותר
description: משרד ההגנה הכריז שאנתרופיק מהווה “סיכון לשרשרת האספקה”, יום לפני שצבא ארה"ב השתמש בטכנולוגיה שלה כדי לתקוף את איראן. התיוג החריג הזה מאיים על עתיד חברת ה-AI המבטיחה ביותר בארה"ב
title_he: הפנטגון מאיים על עתיד כוכבת ה-AI הזוהרת ביותר
summary_he: הפנטגון הכריז על אנתרופיק כגורם פוטנציאלי לסכן את שרשרת האספקה הביטחונית של ארצות הברית, בצעד שלא נראה קודם לכן. הזהירות עלולה לפגוע ביכולתה של החברה להתרחב. הכרזה זו נחשבת כצעד דרמטי כלפי חברה אמריקאית מובילה.
details_he: הכרזה של הפנטגון נחשבת לצעד חסר תקדים כלפי חברה אמריקאית. כלים דומים הופעלו בעבר כלפי חברות זרות שנתפסו כאיום ביטחוני. הזהירות עלולה לפגוע ביכולתה של אנתרופיק להתרחב בקרב חברות המספקות שירותי צד שלישי. הכרזה זו גם עלולה לפגוע באמון של חברות אלה בחברה.

title_original: טראמפ אסר שימוש בקלוד, אך הוא כיכב במתקפה על איראן
description: שעות לאחר שהנשיא הורה להפסיק את ההתקשרויות עם החברה ולהגדירה כסיכון בשרשרת האספקה, נעשה שימוש במודל קלוד בתקיפה באיראן • במקביל חתמה OpenAI על הסכם חדש עם הפנטגון, בצל ביטול החוזים עם אנתרופיק
title_he: טראמפ אסר שימוש בקלוד, אך הוא כיכב במתקפה על איראן

title_original: קיבלתם סמס מפיקוד העורף? תיזהרו - מדובר בקישור זדוני
description: מערך הסייבר הלאומי מזהיר מהודעות SMS המתחזות לפיקוד העורף ומכילות קישורים זדוניים. הקישורים מפנים להורדת אפליקציות מזיקות שעלולות לאפשר גישה למידע אישי • המערך ממליץ: לא ללחוץ על קישורים, לעדכן אפליקציות רק דרך Google Play ו-App Store, ולבדוק עדכונים רק בערוצים הרשמיים של פיקוד העורף • במקרה של פעילות חשודה ניתן לפנות למוקד 119
title_he: קיבלתם סמס מפיקוד העורף? תיזהרו - מדובר בקישור זדוני
summary_he: בישראל, מערך הסייבר הלאומי מזהיר מהודעות SMS שמתחזות לפיקוד העורף. הודעות אלו כוללות קישורים זדוניים שעלולים לגרום להתקנת אפליקציות מזיקות. זה עלול לאפשר גישה למידע אישי.
details_he: המערך ממליץ לאזרחי ישראל לא ללחוץ על קישורים שמגיעים בהודעות SMS. יש לעדכן אפליקציות רק דרך Google Play ו-App Store, ולבדוק עדכונים רק בערוצים הרשמיים של פיקוד העורף. במקרה של פעילות חשודה, ניתן לפנות למוקד 119.

title_original: אין לכם קליטה בממ"ד? זה מה שאפשר לעשות
description: כשנמצאים זמן רב בממ"ד, רבים עשויים להתקשות עם הקליטה הסלולרית • מנקודת גישה בתוך הממ"ד, דרך מתאם רשת חשמל ועד טכנולוגיית ה-Mesh - אלו הפתרונות שקיימים בשוק וכדאי להכיר

title_original: בזמן ההפצצות: מתקפת הסייבר באפליקציית התפילות הפופולרית באיראן
description: במקביל לגל התקיפות, מיליוני איראנים קיבלו דרך אפליקציית תפילות מסרים המבטיחים חנינה לחיילים שיניחו את נשקם • בישראל שומרים על עמימות, אך ההערכה היא כי מדובר במבצע מתוכנן היטב
title_he: בזמן ההפצצות: מתקפת הסייבר באפליקציית התפילות הפופולרית באיראן
summary_he: בעת ההפצצות, אפליקציית תפילות פופולרית באיראן שלחה מיליוני מסרים הקוראים לחיילים לנטוש את נשקם, ומבטיחים חנינה. המסרים נשלחו בזמן שהגל התקיפות הסייבר התרחש. ההערכה היא כי מדובר במבצע מתוכנן.
details_he: בעת ההפצצות, אפליקציית תפילות פופולרית באיראן שלחה מיליוני מסרים הקוראים לחיילים לנטוש את נשקם, ומבטיחים חנינה. המסרים נשלחו בזמן שהגל התקיפות הסייבר התרחש. ההערכה היא כי מדובר במבצע מתוכנן, ובישראל שומרים על עמימות. האפליקציה הפופולרית, שמספר משתמשיה הוא מיליוני, נחשדת בהפצת המסרים.

title_original: טסלה מציעה לראשונה טרייד-אין בישראל
description: לפני קצת יותר מ-5 שנים נכנסה Tesla לשוק הישראלי. על אף שהיא הספיקה להפוך למכונית החשמלית הכי נמכרת בישראל וגם לצאת לרכבת הרים עם המחירים שלה, רק בשנת 2026 היא מתחילה פעילות טרייד-אין גם בישראל – אבל בדרך שונה. לקבל פחות על הרכב המשומש, אבל לחסוך כאב ראש עסקאות טרייד-אין בענף הרכב מאפשרות לכם לחסוך […]The postטסלה מציעה לראשונה טרייד-אין בישראלappeared first onגיקטיים.
title_he: טסלה מציעה לראשונה טרייד-אין בישראל
summary_he: Tesla, the electric vehicle company, has started offering trade-in services in Israel for the first time, but with a significant difference from how it works in other countries. The service allows customers to trade in their old vehicles for a new one, but with a twist - they will receive a lower price for their old vehicle, but with less hassle.
details_he: Tesla has been operating in Israel for over five years and has become the best-selling electric vehicle in the country. However, until now, it did not offer trade-in services. The company has now partnered with 'Trade Mobil' to offer trade-in services in Israel, but with a different approach than in other countries. In Israel, customers will not be able to trade in their old vehicles directly with Tesla, but will need to go through a third-party platform. This means that customers will not receive the same benefits as in other countries, such as being able to trade in their old vehicle directly with Tesla and receiving a higher price for it. Instead, they will receive a lower price for their old vehicle, but with less hassle.
